text: Available scientific evidence does not support any link between vaccines and autism. The original study claiming a link was found to be fraudulent and was retracted. Large population studies have found no association between the MMR vaccine and autism.
The MMR vaccine is highly effective at preventing measles, mumps, and rubella. Two doses of MMR vaccine are about 97% effective at preventing measles; one dose is about 93% effective.

Viruses cannot travel on radio waves or mobile networks. COVID-19 is spread through respiratory droplets and close contact, not by 5G or any mobile network technology. COVID-19 spreads in many countries that do not have 5G networks.

Disinfectants and bleach are for cleaning surfaces only. They must not be ingested, injected, or used on the body to treat or prevent COVID-19, as they cause serious harm including poisoning and death.

COVID-19 vaccines are safe and effective at reducing the risk of severe illness, hospitalization, and death. They went through rigorous clinical trials and continuous safety monitoring. COVID-19 vaccines do not alter human DNA.

Antibiotics do not work against viruses such as those causing colds, flu, or most sore throats. Antibiotics only treat bacterial infections. Taking antibiotics for viral infections does not help and contributes to antibiotic resistance.

Misuse and overuse of antibiotics are driving the development of antibiotic-resistant bacteria. Antibiotics should only be used when prescribed by a certified health professional, and the full course should be completed as directed.

Regular vitamin C supplementation does not reduce the risk of getting the common cold in the general population and does not cure colds. It may slightly shorten the duration of cold symptoms in some people. Megadoses do not provide additional benefit and can cause side effects.

There is no good scientific evidence that an alkaline diet can cure or prevent cancer. The body tightly regulates blood pH regardless of diet; food cannot meaningfully change blood acidity. Cancer is treated with evidence-based therapies such as surgery, chemotherapy, and radiotherapy.

There is no scientific evidence that detox diets, cleanses, or special teas remove toxins from the body or prevent disease. The liver and kidneys remove waste products naturally. So-called detox products are not a substitute for medical care.

Microwaving food does not make it radioactive and does not destroy all its nutrients. Microwave cooking can actually preserve nutrients well because it uses shorter cooking times and less water than some other methods.
Controlled scientific studies have not found that sugar causes hyperactivity in children. The belief persists despite multiple double-blind trials showing no effect of sugar on children's behavior.
Research has not found that cracking your knuckles causes arthritis. Studies comparing habitual knuckle-crackers with non-crackers found no difference in rates of hand arthritis.

Smoking tobacco causes lung cancer and is responsible for about 90% of lung cancer deaths. Smoking also causes cancers of the mouth, throat, esophagus, bladder, and other organs, as well as heart disease and stroke.

Regular physical activity reduces the risk of cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, and several cancers. Adults should do at least 150 to 300 minutes of moderate-intensity aerobic activity per week.

Handwashing with soap and water removes germs and reduces the spread of respiratory and diarrheal infections. Regular handwashing is one of the most effective ways to prevent the spread of infectious disease.

Sunscreen helps protect the skin from ultraviolet (UV) radiation, which damages skin and increases the risk of skin cancer. Broad-spectrum sunscreen should be combined with shade and protective clothing.

Taking folic acid before and during early pregnancy reduces the risk of neural tube defects such as spina bifida. People who can become pregnant are advised to take 400 micrograms of folic acid daily.

Insulin is a hormone used to manage blood glucose levels. People with type 1 diabetes require insulin to survive, and some people with type 2 diabetes also need it. Diabetes cannot be cured by stopping medication or by unproven herbal remedies alone.

High blood pressure (hypertension) is a major risk factor for stroke, heart attack, and kidney disease. It often has no symptoms, so regular blood pressure checks and treatment when needed are important.

HIV is transmitted through blood, semen, vaginal fluids, and breast milk. It is not transmitted by casual contact such as hugging, shaking hands, sharing food, mosquito bites, or using the same toilet. Effective antiretroviral treatment allows people with HIV to live long, healthy lives.

Malaria is caused by Plasmodium parasites transmitted through the bites of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes. It is not caused by eating oily food or by cold weather. Malaria is preventable with insecticide-treated nets and treatable with antimalarial medicines.

Oral rehydration solution (ORS) is a safe, effective, low-cost treatment for dehydration caused by diarrhea. Combined with zinc supplementation, ORS prevents deaths from dehydration, especially in children.

Sickle cell disease is an inherited blood disorder caused by abnormal hemoglobin. It is not contagious and cannot be caught from another person. It is managed with medical care, hydration, pain management, and treatments such as hydroxyurea.

WHO recommends exclusive breastfeeding for the first six months of life, with continued breastfeeding alongside appropriate complementary foods up to two years or beyond. Breast milk provides ideal nutrition and protects infants against infections.

Mental health conditions such as depression and anxiety are common, treatable medical conditions, not signs of personal weakness or spiritual failing. Effective treatments include psychological therapies and, where appropriate, medication.
Vaccines train the immune system to recognize and fight specific pathogens. They do not overwhelm or weaken the immune system, and receiving multiple vaccines does not harm a child's immune system.

WHO recommends against the use of ivermectin to treat or prevent COVID-19 except within clinical trials. The current evidence does not show that ivermectin reduces deaths, hospitalizations, or time to recovery from COVID-19.

Typhoid fever is caused by Salmonella Typhi bacteria spread through contaminated food and water. It is prevented through safe water, sanitation, hygiene, and vaccination, and treated with appropriate antibiotics.

Overweight and obesity result from an energy imbalance between calories consumed and expended, influenced by diet, physical activity, and environment. There is no single food or supplement that safely 'melts fat' without dietary and lifestyle change.
The flu vaccine cannot give you the flu; the viruses in flu shots are inactivated or there are no live viruses at all. The vaccine reduces the risk of flu illness, hospitalization, and death.
Drinking water supports normal body functions and helps prevent dehydration. Most adults need around 6 to 8 glasses of fluid a day, with more needed in hot weather or during exercise.
The claim that humans use only 10 percent of their brains is a myth. Brain imaging shows that virtually all parts of the brain have known functions and are active at different times.
